item.title: City mega transport projects go slow
item.description: Different mega projects to improve the capital city’s communication system are being implemented at a snail’s pace....

item.title: Grameen Kalyan launches free cervical, breast cancer screening initiative
item.description: The Grameen Kalyan has recently launched a healthcare initiative providing 20,000 free cervical and clinical breast cancer screenings to underprivileged women across the country....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Indonesia orangutan forest cleared for ‘carbon-neutral’ packaging firm
item.description: Vast tracts of Indonesian rainforest home to endangered orangutans have been cleared for plantations supplying a maker of ‘carbon-neutral’ packaging, an investigation by AFP and The Gecko Project has found....International news coverage

item.title: Wealthy Gulf states in limbo as US-Iran crisis drags on
item.description: After weathering unprecedented attacks from Iran, the wealthy Gulf states are now stuck between war and peace as talks stall and the vital Strait of Hormuz remains all but closed — threatening economic recovery....

item.title: Chinese EVs, flying cars take centre stage
item.description: Thousands squeezed into the world’s biggest car show Friday, snapping selfies beside Chinese electric vehicles as automakers showcased AI, humanoid robots and flying-car ambitions in a cut-throat market....

item.title: ASUS showcases AI-powered laptops
item.description: ASUS Bangladesh on Wednesday hosted its ‘Always Incredible’ event in the capital, where the company showcased a new generation of AI-powered laptops designed for work, creativity and everyday life, said a press release....

item.title: AGM of Islami Bank Capital Management held
item.description: Islami Bank Capital Management Limited organised its 16th annual general meeting on Thursday at Islami Bank Tower in the capital, said a press release....Bangladesh business insights

item.description: State minister for textiles and jute Md Shariful Alam visited Ghorashal Industrial Park of PRAN-RFL Group in Narsingdi on Tuesday, said a press release....

item.description: Revoo Bangladesh on Sunday opened its newest showroom ‘Labib Motors’ at Mohammadpur in the capital, said a press release....

item.title: CFMOTO launches EV brand ZEEHO
item.description: CFMOTO, manufacturer of high-quality motorcycles, off-road vehicles, engines and accessories, launched its new electric vehicle brand ZEEHO along with its new motorcycle lineup at the 10th Dhaka Bike Show 2026, held at the International Convention City Bashundhara in the capital on Friday....

item.title: DeepSeek releases long-awaited new AI model
item.description: Chinese startup DeepSeek released a new artificial intelligence model with ‘drastically reduced’ costs Friday, more than a year after it stunned the world with a low-cost reasoning model that matched the capabilities of US rivals....

item.title: BCCCI signs deal with Alibaba.com
item.description: The Bangladesh China Chamber of Commerce and Industry has recently signed an agreement with Alibaba.com at the chamber’s office at Gulshan in the capital, said a press release....Geographic Reference

item.title: LNG markets to remain strained thru 2027: IEA
item.description: Liquefied natural gas are likely to remain strained through the end of 2027 due to disruptions and infrastructure damage from the US-Iran war, the International Energy Agency said Friday....

item.title: Exporters welcome BB decision, eye trade boost
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has reintroduced low-interest pre-shipment credit for exporters, aiming to reinvigorate export trade amid global economic uncertainties. ...

item.title: Spiking commodity prices strain consumers
item.description: Prices of chicken, eggs, sugar and vegetables remained high on Friday, alongside a persistent shortage of edible oils, leaving consumers under continued pressure....

item.title: Projecting Bangladesh properly
item.description: WHEN people hear of Brazil, they think of football and festivals. Switzerland brings to mind precision, peace and chocolates. Japan symbolises discipline and innovation. But when people hear f Bangladesh, what comes to mind? Flood and factories?......

item.title: Reading between ceasefires
item.description: HOURS before the truce was set to expire on April 22, US president Donald J Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran once again, this time indefinitely, reportedly at Pakistan’s request. It was the latest in a series of unilateral extensions. The initial......

item.title: Time to run renewable energy system
item.description: THE Iran–America–Israel conflict has already begun to create ripple effects far beyond the battlefield and Bangladesh is feeling the pressure. The global energy market has turned volatile, and as a result, the government is facing a difficult situation. Fuel prices have been......

item.title: See-saw politics
item.description: DHAKA has a new favourite joke — Bangladeshi governments’ promises and commitments change faster than students change Facebook profile pictures — quickly, visibly and accompanied by declarations that things will change. But patterns always repeat....Bangladesh business insights

item.title: Safety failures demand institutional accountability
item.description: A PRE-DAWN excavation collapse at a major urban infrastructure site has once again exposed the dangerous convergence of weak safety oversight, contractor opacity and emergency response fragility in the public works sector. During soil examination......

item.title: A failure that risks normalising impunity
item.description: THE failure to ensure justice even 13 years after the collapse of Rana Plaza, one of the deadliest industrial disasters in modern history, is deplorable and consequences of this failure are profound. The disaster at Savar, which left 1,136 workers dead and......

item.description: China’s new supply chain regulations could be a ‘concern’ for US firms, the American Chamber of Commerce in China warned on Thursday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Russia cuts interest rate after Putin warns on slow growth
item.description: Russia on Friday trimmed its benchmark interest rate, days after president Vladimir Putin sounded the alarm over a slowing economy and ordered his officials to boost growth....

item.title: Young Americans try digital detox
item.description: Getting around without Google Maps. No longer scrolling Instagram at the bus stop. Ditching your headphones to hear the birds sing....

item.title: Canadian AI startup Cohere to acquire Germany’s Aleph Alpha
item.description: Canadian artificial intelligence start-up Cohere will acquire German AI firm Aleph Alpha to offer governments and firms ‘sovereign’ alternatives to huge US and Chinese tech firms, officials said Friday....

item.description: OpenAI released a new model it touts as its best yet for handling research work like making improved versions of itself, as rapid-fire releases by AI rivals pick up pace....

item.title: Tesla starts ‘robotaxi’ production: Musk
item.description: Tesla’s much-touted autonomous ‘robotaxi’, called the Cybercab, has started production, CEO Elon Musk said on Friday, the same week that the carmaker reported first-quarter profits that beat expectations....

item.title: Japan inflation heats up in March, energy prices fall
item.description: Japan’s core inflation accelerated to 1.8 per cent in March, government data showed Friday, largely driven by rising food prices....

item.title: Meta plans 10pc layoffs as AI spending soars
item.description: Meta plans to cut a tenth of its workforce, looking for productivity gains from its remaining workers as it invests heavily in artificial intelligence....

item.description: Oil prices slid Friday on hopes of fresh peace talks aimed at ending the Middle East war, with Iran’s foreign minister expected later in Islamabad....

item.description: The police on Friday arrested seven leaders and activists of banned Bangladesh Chhatra League after leaders and activists of the organisation brought out three flash processions in the Chattogram city on the day....Environmental news updates

item.description: Local residents on Friday demanded the cancellation of Kalma union parishad member Momin Uddin Hiron’s membership for allegedly conducting a secret arbitration over a rape incident at Louhajang in Munshiganj....

item.description: The police cancelled the approval for holding a week-long book fair at Sylhet Central Shaheed Minar hours before its inauguration, fearing a deterioration of the law and order situation....Geographic Reference

item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University has secured first position among the country’s agricultural universities, securing a 401–500 position, in the 2026 Asia University Rankings published by Times Higher Education....Breaking news alerts

item.description: At least four alleged robbers were detained by local people and police on Thursday night while impersonating detective branch officials to kidnap two gold graders at Musharia village of Kotalipara upazila in Gopalganj district....

item.description: Leaders and activists of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party allegedly assaulted a college principal and a female teacher at Daokandi Government College in Durgapur upazila of Rajshahi on Thursday....

item.description: The International Standard University has published the first issue of its textile-based magazine Spandex, aiming to nurture students’ creativity, innovative thinking and presentation skills....

item.description: India on Thursday criticised as inappropriate a post by US president Donald Trump that called the South Asian country a ‘hellhole.’...

item.title: Movement to go on until referendum verdict executed: Jamaat-led alliance
item.description: Leaders of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance on Friday at a rally in Dhaka said that they would wage tougher movements to compel the Bangladesh Nationalist Party- led government to implement the verdict of referendum held on February 12. ...

item.description: Farmers across the Khulna region have begun harvesting Boro paddy in full swing, driven by concerns that natural calamities, including cyclonic storms, could damage their crops....

item.description: At least four people, including a three-year-old boy, were killed and 26 others were injured in road accidents in Gopalgnaj, Narail, Chattogram, Cox’s Bazar, and Jhenaidah on Friday....

item.description: Bus services on long-distance, inter-district and intra-upazila routes from Chapainawabganj remained suspended on Friday following a dispute between two transport worker organisations over toll collection from vehicles, causing severe suffering to passengers....

item.description: A worker died after construction material fell on his head while working on the seventh floor of an under-construction building in the capital’s Mohammadpur area on Friday....

item.title: Bangladesh among 10 countries with two-thirds of acute hunger
item.description: Ten countries, including Bangladesh, accounted for two-thirds of all people facing high levels of acute hunger, said a global report released on Friday....

item.title: Measles outbreak caused by low jabbing: WHO
item.description: The ongoing measles outbreak in Bangladesh is being fuelled by suboptimal population immunity, with most deaths occurring among the unvaccinated children under two years of age, according to the latest Disease Outbreak News released by the World Health Organization....Environmental news updates

item.title: People suffer amid power cuts, heatwave
item.description: People across Bangladesh endured a sweeping heatwave on Friday, as the country experienced up to 1,818 megawatts of load-shedding in different districts, worsening public suffering, officials said....

item.title: Pakistan bids to revive US-Iran talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon extended their shaky ceasefire by three weeks on Friday, as Iran’s foreign minister prepared for meetings with officials in Pakistan, which has been mediating efforts to end the wider Middle East war....
